nodes.text: What are the causes of long covid and what are the best treatments?
There is no consensus on the causes of long covid or on the best treatments.

Persistent symptoms of long covid are associated with female sex, higher deprivation, pre-existing health conditions, more severe acute Covid-19 symptoms and infection with the original “Wild-type” SARS-Cov-2 variant.

persistent symptoms were associated with female sex, higher deprivation, pre-existing health conditions, more severe acute Covid-19 symptoms and being infected with the original “Wild-type” SARS-Cov-2 variant.
Prolonged symptoms after Covid-19 are associated with female sex, white ethnicity, poor pre-pandemic health, obesity and asthma.
prolonged symptoms after Covid-19 were associated with female sex, white ethnicity, poor pre-pandemic health, obesity and asthma.
Long covid is associated with female sex, non-white ethnicity, obesity and pre-existing medical conditions, including anxiety and type 2 diabetes.
long covid was associated with female sex, non-white ethnicity, obesity and pre-existing medical conditions, including anxiety and type 2 diabetes.
Multidisciplinary care and the involvement of people with lived experience are important in the design and evaluation of long covid services.
highlighting the importance of multidisciplinary care and involving people with lived experience in the design and evaluation of long covid services.
The REACT Long COVID study uses surveys and interviews to understand experiences and why some people are affected by longer-term symptoms.
REACT Long COVID: this study is gathering data using surveys and interviews to help researchers understand experiences of long covid and why some people are affected by longer-term symptoms.
The Convalescence study uses various research methods to explore definitions, risk factors, and consequences of long covid.
Convalescence: this study is using a variety of research methods to explore definitions of long covid, risk factors and consequences of the condition
The STIMULATE-ICP study assesses integrated care pathways and tests the effectiveness of common medicines for treating long covid.

STIMULATE-ICP: this study is using a range of research methods to assess integrated care pathways for patients with long covid and to test common medicines to see if they are effective in treating long covid.
The LOCOMOTION study examined existing models of care to develop a gold standard for long covid care in different settings.
LOCOMOTION: this study used a range of research methods to examine existing models of care for patients with long covid to develop a ‘gold standard’ for care in different settings.
Does a patient need to have had a positive Covid-19 test to be referred to another service or to receive specialist input or investigations?
a patient does not have to have had a positive Covid-19 test to be referred to another service or to receive specialist input or investigations
A patient does not have to have had a positive Covid-19 test to be referred to another service or to receive specialist input or investigations.
a patient does not have to have had a positive Covid-19 test to be referred to another service or to receive specialist input or investigations
How should long covid be managed and rehabilitated?
NICE guidance focuses on self-management and rehabilitation for people who are affected by long covid.
NICE guidance focuses on self-management and rehabilitation for people who are affected by the condition
There are currently no known effective treatments that can cure long covid.
There are currently no known effective treatments that can cure long covid
Are there adequate healthcare services available for people with long covid?
Some patients have been unable to access appropriate help for long covid.
some patients had been unable to access appropriate help
The UK Government has invested £314 million and established over 100 specialised services to provide care for people with long covid.
the UK Government states it has invested £314 million to provide care for people with long covid, and has established over 100 specialised services
Should NHS England publish a plan to deal with long covid?
NHS England should publish a plan to deal with long covid
NHS England will work with stakeholders to develop and publish a new Long COVID Plan for 2022 to 2023.
NHS England will work with stakeholders, including patient groups, to develop and publish a new Long COVID Plan for 2022 to 2023
Long covid is likely to have implications for demand and workforce across a range of services.
long covid is likely to have implications for demand and workforce across a range of services
How many people are affected by long covid?
How many people are affected by long covid?
In March 2023, an estimated 1.9 million people in the UK reported that they were experiencing long covid, representing 2.9% of the population.
In March 2023, an estimated 1.9 million people in the UK reported that they were experiencing long covid, representing 2.9% of the population.

How has long covid affected healthcare services?
What are the causes of long covid?
Research exploring the causes of the condition and potential treatment options is ongoing.
Research exploring the causes of the condition and potential treatment options is ongoing.
Does long covid count in law as a disability?
Whether or not long covid counts in law as a disability
Long covid is considered a disability in law if it has a substantial effect on day-to-day activities and lasts or is likely to last at least 12 months.

In cases where long covid has a substantial effect on someone’s ability to carry out day-to-day activities and where it lasts or seems likely to last at least 12 months, it will be considered a disability in law.

The legal definition of a disability under section 6 of the Equality Act 2010 is an impairment with a substantial and long-term adverse effect on a person's ability to carry out normal day-to-day activities.

the legal definition of a disability, given in section 6 of the Equality Act 2010, is an impairment that has a “substantial” and “long-term” adverse effect on someone’s “ability to carry out normal day-to-day activities”.
Employers should focus on the reasonable adjustments they can make rather than trying to determine if an employee's condition constitutes a disability.
Employers should focus on the reasonable adjustments they can make rather than trying to work out if an employee's condition is a disability.
What extra flexibility can be offered to workers with long covid?
what extra flexibility can be offered to workers with long covid
The Government is expanding the right to request flexible working through the Employment Relations (Flexible Working) Act 2023.
plans to expand the right to request flexible working, some of which were being delivered by the Employment Relations (Flexible Working) Act 2023
What financial support is available to people unable to work due to long covid, particularly former key workers?
what financial support is available to people unable to work due to long covid, particularly former key workers
The Government does not plan to introduce additional special support specifically for key workers with long covid.
the Government had no plans to introduce additional special support for key workers with long covid

Financial support via Universal Credit, New Style Employment and Support Allowance, and Personal Independence Payment is already available to those unable to work due to long covid regardless of their status as a key worker.

The DWP already offers financial support through Universal Credit and New Style Employment and Support Allowance for people who are unable to work due to long-Covid, subject to them satisfying the eligibility criteria and contribution conditions. In addition, people with Long-Covid may be eligible for Personal Independence Payment. This support is available regardless of keyworker status.
Should long covid be prescribed as an occupational disease for the purposes of Industrial Injuries Disablement Benefit?
Long covid is not currently prescribed as an occupational disease for the purposes of Industrial Injuries Disablement Benefit
The Industrial Injuries Advisory Council recommends that specific long covid complications be added to the list of prescribed diseases for IIDB entitlement.
IIAC recommended the following be added to the list of prescribed diseases allowing entitlement to IIDB
The IIAC found convincing and consistent evidence that five serious pathological complications of long covid cause persistent impairment and loss of function in health and social workers.

IIAC found "convincing and consistent evidence" of five serious pathological complications for health and social workers with long covid. These complications had been shown to cause persistent impairment and loss of function in some workers.
What are the causes of long covid?
There are likely multiple, potentially overlapping causes of long covid.
there are likely multiple, potentially overlapping, causes of long covid
There may be multiple subtypes of long covid associated with different symptoms and underlying causes.
there may be multiple subtypes of long covid associated with different symptoms and underlying causes
Persistent infection with the SARS-CoV-2 virus, which causes Covid-19, may be a cause.
Persistent infection with the SARS-CoV-2 virus, which causes Covid-19
Immune system problems, including the reactivation of dormant viruses or the development of an autoimmune response, may be a cause.
Immune system problems, including the reactivation of other viruses that may be dormant in the body, or the development of an autoimmune response, where the body attacks its own systems
Changes to the microbiota, the microorganisms that live on and in the human body, may be a cause.
Changes to the microbiota, the microorganisms that live on and in the human body
Problems with blood clotting and changes to endothelial cells inside blood vessels may be a cause.
Problems with blood clotting and changes to endothelial cells, which are found inside blood vessels
Problems with the nervous system, including the brainstem or vagus nerve, may be a cause.
Problems with the nervous system, including in the brainstem or vagus nerve, which carries signals between the brain, heart and digestive system
How many people are affected by long covid?
How many people are affected by long covid?
In March 2023, an estimated 1.9 million people in the UK were experiencing self-reported long covid, representing 2.9% of the population.
In March 2023, an estimated 1.9 million people in the UK were experiencing self-reported long covid, representing 2.9% of the population
What are the symptoms of long covid?
People with longer-term effects after a Covid-19 infection can experience a wide range of symptoms affecting different parts of the body.
People with longer-term effects after a Covid-19 infection can experience a wide range of symptoms that can affect different parts of the body.
Symptoms include respiratory, cardiovascular, generalised, neurological, gastrointestinal, musculoskeletal, ear, nose and throat, dermatological, and psychological or psychiatric symptoms.

Respiratory symptoms, such as breathlessness or a cough
- Cardiovascular symptoms, such as chest tightness or pain, or palpitations
- Generalised symptoms, including fatigue, fever (a temperature over 38°C) or pain
- Neurological symptoms, including cognitive impairment (sometimes termed ‘brain fog’), headaches, disrupted sleep, peripheral neuropathy (pins and needles or numbness in the hands or feet), dizziness, delirium, mobility impairment or visual disturbances
- Gastrointestinal symptoms, including pain, nausea, vomiting, diarrhoea, weight loss and reduced appetite
- Musculoskeletal symptoms, such as joint or muscle pain
- Ear, nose and throat symptoms, such as tinnitus (hearing sounds that do not come from an outside source), earache, sore throat, the loss of taste and/or smell, nasal congestion (blocked nose)
- Dermatological symptoms, such as skin rashes or hair loss
- Psychological or psychiatric symptoms, such as depression, anxiety or symptoms of post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD).
Some symptoms of long covid are less common in children and young people.
some symptoms are less common in children and young people
Children may be less affected by respiratory symptoms, such as shortness of breath and cough, and cardiovascular symptoms, such as palpitations or pain.
children may be less affected by respiratory symptoms (including shortness of breath and cough) and cardiovascular symptoms (including palpitations or pain).
How should long covid be assessed and investigated?
NICE recommends that patients are assessed using a holistic, person-centred approach.
NICE recommends that patients are assessed using a “holistic, person-centred approach”.
Assessment should include a comprehensive clinical history and an examination of physical, cognitive, psychological, and psychiatric symptoms, as well as the ability to perform daily tasks.

This assessment should include taking a comprehensive clinical history (including the history of known or suspected Covid-19 infections and other health conditions) and examining the patient to assess their physical, cognitive, psychological and psychiatric symptoms, as well as their ability to carry out day-to-day tasks.
Tests and investigations should be offered based on symptoms to rule out serious complications of Covid-19 and alternative diagnoses.
tests and investigations should be offered to patients based on their symptoms, with the aim of ruling out serious complications of Covid-19 infection and alternative diagnoses.
Investigations may include blood tests, exercise tolerance tests, chest x-rays, or patient self-monitoring of heart rate, blood pressure, and pulse oximetry.

These might include blood tests, exercise tolerance tests or chest x-rays (for continuing respiratory symptoms), or patients’ self-monitoring of heart rate, blood pressure, pulse oximetry, or ongoing symptoms.
When should patients be referred to other services?
Clinicians should consider referring patients to an appropriate service, such as an integrated multidisciplinary assessment service.
clinicians should consider referring people to “an appropriate service, such as an integrated multidisciplinary assessment service”.
Referral should be considered when serious complications of Covid-19 and alternative diagnoses have been ruled out.
When serious complications of Covid-19 and alternative diagnoses have been ruled out
A clinician could refer a patient to an assessment service any time after four weeks have passed since the start of an acute Covid-19 infection.
A clinician could refer a patient to an assessment service at any time after four weeks have passed since the start of an acute Covid-19 infection.
What is the impact of long covid on healthcare services?
the impact of long covid on healthcare services
The impact of long covid on healthcare services centres on the cost of providing services for patients and the effect on the NHS workforce.
Concerns about the impact of long covid on healthcare services centre on the cost of providing services for long covid patients and the impact of long covid on the NHS workforce
The British Medical Association has called for financial support and improved services for healthcare staff affected by long covid to address the impact on the workforce.

The British Medical Association, the trade union for doctors, has called for a range of measures, including financial support and improved services for doctors and healthcare staff affected by long covid
Whether long covid is a disability
Long covid has had a widespread impact on the workforce.
the widespread impact that long covid has had on the workforce
The Society of Occupational Medicine suggests that organisations should review absence management and flexible working practices to support employees affected by long covid.
The Society of Occupational Medicine... called for organisations to review their absence management and flexible working practices to help support employees
Does long covid affect children's school attendance?
long covid can affect children’s school attendance
Children affected by long covid may require additional support to access full-time education.
affected children may need additional support to access full-time education
There are concerns regarding increased absence from school since the relaxation of pandemic restrictions.
there have been concerns about increased absence from school
NICE provides specific case definitions to help clinicians identify and diagnose the longer-term effects of Covid-19.
The NICE guidance includes the following case definitions to help clinicians to identify and diagnose longer-term effects of Covid-19
Post-Covid-19 syndrome is diagnosed when symptoms continue for more than 12 weeks and cannot be explained by another diagnosis.
Post-Covid-19 syndrome: signs and symptoms that develop during or after an infection consistent with Covid-19, that continue for more than 12 weeks, and are not explained by an alternative diagnosis
The World Health Organization has published its own clinical case definition for post-Covid-19 condition.
the World Health Organization (WHO) published a clinical case definition of post-Covid-19 condition
The WHO definition is similar to the definition adopted by NICE for post-Covid-19 syndrome.
The definition proposed by WHO is similar to the definition adopted by NICE for post-Covid-19 syndrome
Are there concerns regarding the NICE guidance on long covid?
Concerns have also been expressed about the NICE guidance on long covid
The NICE guidance did not adequately describe long covid or its causes.
the guidance did not adequately describe the condition or its causes

The guideline is overly focused on self-management, psychological support, and rehabilitation, which may result in NHS long covid clinics that do not provide thorough physical assessments of patients.

The guideline is overly focused on self-management, psychological support, and rehabilitation, resulting in the potential for ‘watered-down’ versions of NHS long COVID clinics that do not provide thorough physical assessment of patients.

How - and to what extent - did decision makers warn the public about the risk of developing Long Covid and take the disease into account in public health communications
Was it avoidable that nearly 2 million people are suffering from long covid today?
Whether nearly 2 million people suffering from Long Covid today was avoidable?
People were given sufficient advice to protect themselves from covid, and the risks of long covid were publicised as soon as they were understood.
people were given plenty of advice to protect themselves from Covid, and as soon as we began to understood what Long Covid was and its risks, we also publicised the risks of Long Covid as well
Boris Johnson denied that his prolonged disbelief in long covid contributed to a delay in its recognition.
Boris Johnson denied that his “prolonged disbelief” in long covid contributed to a delay in its recognition
Are some groups more likely to experience long covid than others?
some groups are more likely to experience long covid than others
The prevalence of self-reported long covid was greater among female respondents than male respondents.
the prevalence of self-reported long covid was greater among female respondents (3.3%) than male respondents (2.5%)
Prevalence rates of long covid were higher among people aged 35 to 69 than the general population.
Prevalence rates were higher among people aged 35 to 49 (4.2%) and 50 to 69 (4.5%) than the general population (2.9%)
Children and young people under the age of 25 were at the lowest risk of long covid.
Children and young people were least at risk, with rates in those under the age of 25 lower than in all other age groups
People with pre-existing health conditions that limit day-to-day activities were considerably more likely to experience long covid.

people who had a self-reported, preexisting health condition that affects their ability to carry out day-to-day activities, who were considerably more likely to experience long covid than those with a non-limiting health condition or no pre-existing conditions
The prevalence of self-reported long covid was higher among those living in the most deprived areas.
Prevalence of self-reported long covid was higher among those living in the most deprived areas (3.9%)
A higher proportion of people in the White ethnic group reported experiencing long covid than those in Black or Asian ethnic groups.
A higher proportion of people in the White ethnic group reported experiencing long covid (3.0%) than people in the Black (1.7%) or Asian (2.4%) ethnic groups
Prevalence rates of long covid were higher among people working in social and health care than in other employment sectors.
Prevalence rates were also higher among people working in social care (5.3%) and health care (4.4%) than in other employment sectors
The prevalence of long covid was highest among people living in the North East of England and Scotland.
prevalence was highest among those living in the North East of England (3.7%) and in Scotland (3.3%)
How has long covid affected healthcare services?
How has long covid affected healthcare services?
The impact of long covid on healthcare services centres on the cost of providing services and the effect on the NHS workforce.
Concerns about the impact of long covid on healthcare services centre on the cost of providing services for long covid patients and the impact of long covid on the NHS workforce
Between 5,000 and 10,000 NHS staff could be off sick with long covid.
between 5,000 and 10,000 NHS staff could be off sick with long covid
Primary care consultations related to long covid could cost over £23 million per year.
primary care consultations in relation to long covid could cost over £23 million each year
What is the long-term impact of COVID-19 on doctors?
the long-term impact of COVID-19 on doctors
Long covid has a significant personal and professional impact on doctors.
significant personal and professional impact of long covid
18% of survey respondents reported being unable to work, and others reduced their working or training hours.
18% of survey respondents reported that they were left unable to work, and others have reduced their working and/or training hours
The BMA argues that financial support and improved services should be provided for doctors and healthcare staff affected by long covid.
The BMA has called for a range of measures, including financial support and improved services for doctors and healthcare staff affected by long covid
Long covid should be recognised as an occupational disease for healthcare workers.
The BMA has also called for the condition to be recognised as an occupational disease for healthcare workers
What is the widespread impact that long covid is having on the workforce?
the widespread impact that long covid was having on the workforce
Fatigue, cognitive dysfunction, and changes in taste and smell are the symptoms with the greatest impact on work and returning to work.

Long COVID symptoms that seem to have the greatest impact on work and return to work are fatigue, cognitive dysfunction (such as difficulty concentrating and memory loss), and changes in taste and smell
One in four employers include long covid as a main cause of long-term sickness absence.
one in four employers now include Long COVID among their main causes of long-term sickness absence
Returning to work after prolonged sickness absence with long covid requires a tailored, long-term, and flexible approach.
Return to work (RTW) after prolonged sickness absence with Long COVID needs a tailored, long-term and flexible approach
A tailored approach is necessary because long covid is multifactorial, unlike other conditions, making it important to distinguish specific health problems in each case.
A key difference between Long COVID and other conditions is the multifactorial nature of the condition and it is important to distinguish the specific health problems in each case
Working-age people are less likely to participate in the labour market after developing long covid symptoms than before their infection.
working-age people are less likely to participate in the labour market after developing long COVID symptoms than they were before being infected with coronavirus (COVID-19)
What is the potential impact of long covid on the labour market?
the potential impact of long covid on the labour market
The impact of long covid is equivalent to 110,000 workers being off sick on any given day.
the impact of Long covid was equivalent to 110,000 workers being off sick on any given day
Long covid is unlikely to be driving the increase in economic inactivity.
long covid was unlikely to be driving the increase in economic inactivity
If long covid drove economic inactivity, this trend would be observed in other countries with high infection rates.
it would be observed in other countries with high rates of infection during the pandemic
The impact of long covid and NHS waiting lists on economic inactivity is unclear.
The impact of long COVID-19 and NHS waiting lists on economic inactivity is unclear
Should ministers accept the Industrial Injuries Advisory Council's recommendations?
Ministers must now decide whether to accept IIAC’s recommendations
The Department for Work and Pensions is carrying out a detailed assessment of the report's recommendations.
the Department for Work and Pensions (DWP) was carrying out a detailed assessment of the report’s recommendations
Should long covid be included as a prescribed disease?
Debate about including long covid as a prescribed disease
The Industrial Injuries Advisory Council considered that there was not sufficient evidence to recommend general prescription for long covid or post-Covid syndrome.
IIAC therefore considered there was not sufficient evidence at that time to recommend general prescription for long covid/post-Covid syndrome

Current understanding of the underlying pathophysiology, temporal course, and predictable effects of the key symptoms of post-Covid syndrome is limited, and the ability to diagnose the condition objectively is also limited.

current understanding of the underlying pathophysiology, temporal course, and predictable effects of the key symptoms of Post-COVID syndrome is limited, as is the ability to diagnose the condition objectively
The Council is unable to recommend prescription for long covid at this time.
the Council is unable to recommend prescription for this condition at this time
Is there a need for greater recognition and support for long covid?
Calls for greater recognition and support for long covid
Patients, charities, and healthcare professionals believe there is a need for greater recognition and awareness of long covid, as well as better access to medical assessments and support.
Patients, charities and health care professionals have raised concerns about recognition and awareness of long covid, and difficulties in accessing appropriate medical assessments and support
A report highlighted a lack of awareness and knowledge of the condition and support services among GPs, mixed experiences of long covid clinics, and a lack of holistic support.
The report highlighted a lack of awareness and knowledge of the condition and support services amongst GPs, mixed experience of long Covid clinics and a lack of holistic support
